| Curry Powder | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Category | Herbs and spices |

Cookbook | Recipes | Ingredients | Equipment | Techniques | Cookbook Disambiguation Pages | Ingredients

**Curry powder** is a general blend of spices used to imitate the flavor of South Asian cuisine.[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1)[[2]](#cite_note-:1-2) It should not be confused with specific spice blends used in various Indian curries, such as garam masala, chaat masala, panch phoron "Cookbook:Bengali Five-Spice Mix (Panch Puran)"), tikka masala, etc.

## Characteristics

[edit | edit source]

There is no set blend, and every commercial curry powder blend will be slightly different.[[3]](#cite_note-:2-3)[[4]](#cite_note-:3-4) Spices typically found in curry powders include turmeric, coriander, cumin, ginger, cinnamon, cardamom, fenugreek, pepper, and/or chile powder.[[4]](#cite_note-:3-4)[[5]](#cite_note-:4-5)[[6]](#cite_note-:5-6)[[7]](#cite_note-:6-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8) The heat level varies, and the blend may range from mild to hot.[[9]](#cite_note-:7-9)[[2]](#cite_note-:1-2)

## Selection and storage

[edit | edit source]

Like all ground spices, curry powder must be stored in an airtight container at room temperature away from light, heat, and moisture.[[3]](#cite_note-:2-3)[[7]](#cite_note-:6-7)[[10]](#cite_note-10) Still, the flavor and aroma will fade over time, and you should make sure to check it periodically.[[3]](#cite_note-:2-3) Making curry powder fresh is always the best option in terms of flavor.[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1)

## Use

[edit | edit source]

Curry powder is not actually used to make traditional Indian dishes,[[9]](#cite_note-:7-9) which typically rely on more specific spice blends,[[5]](#cite_note-:4-5) and many Indian cooks never use it.[[5]](#cite_note-:4-5)[[6]](#cite_note-:5-6)[[11]](#cite_note-11) Instead, curry powder is used for anglicized Indian dishes like Mulligatawny soup or "curried" Western dishes like curried chicken/egg salad.[[5]](#cite_note-:4-5)[[9]](#cite_note-:7-9) It is also used in Caribbean and various African cuisines influenced by Indian foodways.
